question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the BLS on April 10, 2026. The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis in March 2026, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for a YES resolution. This represents definitive information that the market will resolve to NO.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3%
0.3% is well below the 0.8% threshold needed for YES resolution
The data has been officially released and confirmed by multiple sources
Market should resolve NO with 100% certainty
risks: Potential data revisions (though unlikely to change 0.3% to >0.8%)
Misinterpretation of which CPI measure is used (though CPI-U is standard)
